10.title: A plague without precedent
10.text: The account stresses that neither the fathers nor the fathers' fathers of the Egyptians had ever seen the like — the plague of locusts was portrayed as unprecedented in Egypt's history.

11.title: Silver and gold for the road
11.text: Before leaving, the Israelites would ask the Egyptians for jewels of silver and gold. This 'spoiling' was not theft: it served as long-overdue wages for generations of slave labor, and some of that gold would later be used for the tabernacle.

12.text: By calling this month 'the beginning of months,' the Lord reorders Israel's calendar around the deliverance from Egypt; this month (Abib/Nisan) becomes the start of the religious year.

33.title: Joshua, son of Nun
33.text: The young man Joshua, son of Nun, who did not depart from the tabernacle, would later succeed Moses and lead Israel in conquering the promised land.

34.title: The proclaimed name
34.text: At the top of Sinai the Lord Himself descends in a cloud and proclaims His name, setting up the divine attributes He declares in the verses that follow.

34.title: The shining face
34.text: When Moses came down from Sinai the skin of his face shone because he had spoken with the Lord; that is why he afterward wore a veil before the people.

36.title: Too much given
36.text: This is one of the rare biblical accounts where a leader must ask the people to stop donating: the material they had was sufficient for all the work, and too much.

37.title: The mercy seat
37.text: The mercy seat was the lid of pure gold that covered the ark; above it, between the two cherubim, the Lord manifested His presence and communed with Israel.

38.title: Shittim wood
38.text: The altar of burnt offering was made of shittim (acacia) wood overlaid with brass: acacia, tough and plentiful in the Sinai wilderness, was the wood used for nearly all the tabernacle's furniture.

38.title: Bezaleel and Aholiab
38.text: The work was led by two craftsmen: Bezaleel of the tribe of Judah, and Aholiab of the tribe of Dan, described as an engraver, a cunning workman and an embroiderer.

40.title: A year in the wilderness
40.text: The command to raise the tabernacle falls on the first day of the first month of the second year after leaving Egypt. Nearly a full year passed between the Exodus and the assembling of the sanctuary.

40.template: And thou shalt put therein the ark of the {0}, and cover the ark with the veil.

40.title: The glory fills the tabernacle
40.text: When the work is finished, the cloud covers the tent and the glory of the Lord fills the tabernacle so fully that even Moses cannot enter. It is the same sign of divine presence that would later fill Solomon's temple.

40.template: ¶ Then a {0} covered the tent of the congregation, and the glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle.
